2605.11386.title: Revisiting Privacy Preservation in Brain-Computer Interfaces: Conceptual Boundaries, Risk Pathways, and a Protection-Strength Grading Framework
2605.11386.paper_summary: Brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) are moving rapidly from laboratory research into clinical, edge, and real-world settings. Under ISO/IEC 8663:2025, a BCI is a direct communication link between central nervous system activity and external software or hardware systems. This link expands privacy risk beyond raw neural-signal leakage: neural data, derived representations, model assets, and decoded outputs can be re-associated with individuals across collection, transmission, storage, training, inference, and feedback, or used to infer information beyond what a task requires. Starting from the general BCI paradigm, this review deffnes privacy-protection boundaries, protection objects, and the relationship between user data privacy and model privacy within a shared risk pathway. It then proposes a three-dimensional framework - protection object, lifecycle stage, and dominant protection-strength level - to classify existing work into four levels of protection strength. Finally, mental privacy and neuroethical risks are treated as open issues, emphasizing that BCI privacy protection should not only obscure data but also disentangle task-irrelevant sensitive information while preserving downstream utility. Keywords: Brain-computer interface, Neural data privacy, User data privacy, Model privacy, Disentanglement of task-irrelevant sensitive information, Protection-strength grading, Neuroethical risks
2605.11386.paper_summary_zh: 腦-電腦介面（BCI）正在迅速從實驗室研究轉向臨床、邊緣和現實世界的應用。根據 ISO/IEC 8663:2025，BCI 是中央神經系統活動與外部軟體或硬體系統之間的直接通信連結。這種連結擴大了隱私風險，超出了原始神經信號洩漏的範疇：神經數據、衍生表示、模型資產和解碼輸出可以在收集、傳輸、存儲、訓練、推理和反饋過程中重新與個體關聯，或用於推斷超出任務所需的信息。從一般的 BCI 範式出發，本評述定義了隱私保護邊界、保護對象，以及用戶數據隱私與模型隱私在共享風險路徑中的關係。然後，它提出了一個三維框架 - 保護對象、生命周期階段和主導保護強度水平 - 將現有工作分類為四個保護強度級別。最後，心理隱私和神經倫理風險被視為未解決的問題，強調 BCI 隱私保護不僅應該模糊數據，還應該在保留下游效用的同時，理清與任務無關的敏感信息。關鍵詞：腦-電腦介面、神經數據隱私、用戶數據隱私、模型隱私、與任務無關的敏感信息的解耦、保護強度分級、神經倫理風險
2605.11386.author: Lei Sun et.al.
2605.11386.authors: Lei Sun, Xiuqing Mao, Shuai Zhang, Qingyu Zeng, Min Zhao, Jiyuan Li, Wenle Dong
